evidence: No evidence Sam/Shaan or anyone else built a unified hospitality/clubs/hotels/events/membership software platform tied to this pitch. Real club-management software companies exist (Clubspot founded 2017, Boulevard, etc.) but none traceable to this specific MFM segment or its hosts.

Native was founded in 2015 by Moiz Ali and acquired by Procter & Gamble for ~$100M in November 2017, well before the August 2019 pitch date — this was a discussion of an already-real, already-acquired brand.

No evidence Shaan/Sam launched a dedicated crypto venture fund following this Sept 2019 pitch. Shaan's later ventures (Milk Road, a crypto newsletter co-founded 2021/sold 2022; All-Access-Fund, a general startup rolling fund launched Sept 2020) are adjacent but not a crypto-focused early-stage investment fund as described.
BarkBox was founded in December 2011 by Carly Strife, Matt Meeker, and Henrik Werdelin, long predating the November 2019 pitch date (BarkBox/Bark later went public via SPAC in 2020).
Rev was founded around 2010 by former oDesk employees as a freelance transcription/captioning marketplace, with ~50,000 active freelancers by 2019 — well established before the January 2020 pitch.

Could not identify the specific failed online math tutoring venture referenced in this Jan 2020 episode via web search; no matching startup or founder account surfaced tying a shut-down math tutoring product to an MFM guest.

Webflow was founded in 2012-2013 by Vlad Magdalin, Sergie Magdalin, and Bryant Chou, launched via Y Combinator Summer 2013, and had already raised a $72M Series A by August 2019 — well before the January 2020 pitch.

Everlywell was founded in 2014-2015 by Julia Cheek, launched out of beta in 2015, and had appeared on Shark Tank in 2017 — an established at-home lab testing company well before the February 2020 pitch.

Lucid Drone Technologies was founded in 2018 and went through Y Combinator's Summer 2019 batch (autonomous window-washing/cleaning drones), predating the February 2020 pitch; it has since raised a $20M round and rebranded to Lucid Bots.

No dedicated 'Clout Kitchens' cloud-kitchen-for-food-influencers company was built matching this Feb 2020 pitch. A same-named 'Clout Kitchen' is an unrelated 2024 Manila-based AI gaming-companion startup (Backseat AI), not a cloud kitchen business — coincidental name overlap only.
